Politics.headline: 'This had to be done': Trump claims to have done the world a 'favor' with attacks on Iran
Politics.detail: President Trump stated that the attacks on Iran were a 'favor' to the world, amidst the ongoing conflict between the U.S., Israel, and Iran. Oil prices have soared since the beginning of the conflict.

Politics.headline: Trump tells Starmer help not needed even as US uses UK bases for Iran strikes
Politics.detail: Despite using UK bases for strikes against Iran, President Trump has told Keir Starmer, leader of the UK Labour party, that help is not needed. The US continues its military actions in the Middle East.

Politics.headline: Trump Says No Deal With Iran Except 'Unconditional Surrender'
Politics.detail: President Trump has stated that he will only accept Iran's 'unconditional surrender' as a resolution to the ongoing conflict. This comes as the war continues to impact global oil supplies and geopolitical stability.

Politics.headline: Kristi Noem appears with Trump in new 'Shield of the Americas' role - days after he fired her from DHS
Politics.detail: Just days after being removed from her position as DHS Secretary, Kristi Noem has appeared with President Trump in a new role related to the 'Shield of the Americas' initiative. The details of her new role are still emerging.

Politics.headline: Darrell Issa won't seek reelection after more than two decades in House
Politics.detail: California US Rep. Darrell Issa has announced his retirement after serving for over two decades in the House, raising the stakes for the GOP in maintaining control of the House. This move could have significant implications for the upcoming elections.

Finance & Economy.headline: Oil surges to its highest price since 2023, and stocks drop after data shows a weaker US job market
Finance & Economy.detail: Oil prices have reached their highest level since 2023 due to the Iran war, while a weak U.S. job market update has negatively impacted stocks, marking Wall Street's worst week since October. The combination of a weak economy and high inflation presents challenges for investors as the Federal Reserve lacks effective tools to address both issues simultaneously.

Finance & Economy.headline: US economy shed 92,000 jobs in February, well below expectations
Finance & Economy.detail: The U.S. economy experienced a loss of 92,000 jobs in February, according to new government data, which is significantly lower than anticipated, also investors are worried about oil prices. The Labor Department reported Friday that hiring deteriorated from January, when companies, nonprofits and government agencies added 126,000 jobs.

Finance & Economy.headline: Peso falls to 59:$1 amid Middle East crisis
Finance & Economy.detail: The Philippine peso has depreciated to 59 pesos per U.S. dollar amidst the ongoing crisis in the Middle East. This reflects the economic impact and uncertainty caused by the regional conflict.

Finance & Economy.headline: Industries. Over P20/liter diesel price surge possible on March 10.
Finance & Economy.detail: A potential surge in diesel prices exceeding P20 per liter is anticipated in the Philippines by March 10, 2026. This increase is attributed to rising global oil prices and geopolitical tensions.

Finance & Economy.headline: West Asia conflict threatens India's $11.8 billion agri exports: GTRI
Finance & Economy.detail: The ongoing conflict in West Asia poses a threat to India's agricultural exports, potentially impacting $11.8 billion worth of goods, according to GTRI. The crisis could disrupt trade routes and supply chains, affecting India's agricultural sector.

Science.headline: Bird Droppings Helped Build One of Ancient Peru's Most Powerful Kingdoms
Science.detail: New research suggests seabird guano helped transform the Chincha Kingdom into one of the most prosperous societies in ancient Peru.

Science.headline: Physicists Finally See Strange Magnetic Vortices Predicted 50 Years Ago
Science.detail: Physicists have observed unique magnetic phenomena that they have been trying to observe for decades.

Science.headline: Humanity heating planet faster than ever before, study finds
Science.detail: Researchers identify sharp rise to about 0.35C every decade, after excluding natural fluctuations such as El Niño.

Science.headline: NASA's DART spacecraft changed an asteroid's orbit around the sun
Science.detail: A 2022 NASA mission changed the orbit of the asteroid Dimorphos around its companion, and new data shows their joint orbit around the sun also changed.

Science.headline: Living in space can change where your brain sits in your skull – new research
Science.detail: Going to space is harsh on the human body, and as a new study from our research team finds, the brain shifts upward and backward and deforms inside the skull after spaceflight.

Health.headline: Parent Stress Emerges as Key Factor in Child Obesity
Health.detail: A recent study highlights the significant role of parental stress in the development of child obesity, suggesting interventions should target family-wide well-being.

Health.headline: Pandemic disruptions tied to changes in executive function progress in young children
Health.detail: A recent study shows that the pandemic has caused disruptions that are tied to changes in executive function progress in young children, which can influence memory, academic achievement, relationships, and health.

Health.headline: The 'Golden Hour': Distance and delay define rural trauma care timelines.
Health.detail: A recent study analyzes the impact of distance and delays on trauma care timelines in rural areas, emphasizing the critical importance of the first hour after injury for patient outcomes.

Health.headline: Tylenol use in pregnant women visiting ED dropped after Trump's unfounded autism claim.
Health.detail: Research indicates a decrease in Tylenol use among pregnant women visiting the emergency department following unfounded claims linking the drug to autism, though researchers caution against establishing a direct causal relationship.

Health.headline: Human rights court orders Peru to pay compensation for a 1997 forced sterilization death.
Health.detail: The Inter-American Court of Human Rights has mandated that Peru provide compensation for a death resulting from a forced sterilization procedure that occurred in 1997.

Sports.headline: Marmoush double seals comeback win for Manchester City at Newcastle
Sports.detail: Manchester City secured a comeback victory against Newcastle with a double from Marmoush in their FA Cup match.

Sports.headline: Eze staggers Mansfield and sends Arsenal into FA Cup quarter-finals
Sports.detail: Eze's performance propelled Arsenal into the FA Cup quarter-finals after a compelling match against Mansfield.

Sports.headline: Coventry win again as Millwall close gap on top two
Sports.detail: Coventry continued their winning streak, while Millwall narrowed the gap on the top two teams in the Championship.

Sports.headline: Lakshya Sen after marathon All England win against Victor Lai: 'Plan was to finish off rally in first few shots when I started cramping'
Sports.detail: Lakshya Sen secured a victory at the All England Open against Victor Lai, adjusting his strategy due to cramping.

Sports.headline: 'Wouldn't mind breaking a few hearts to lift the trophy for once': Mitchell Santner ahead of India vs New Zealand T20 World Cup final
Sports.detail: Mitchell Santner expressed his ambition to lead New Zealand to a T20 World Cup victory against India, drawing inspiration from Australia's previous win against India.

Entertainment.headline: Peter Jackson to receive honorary Palme d'Or at Cannes Film Festival 2026
Entertainment.detail: Peter Jackson will be awarded at the Cannes Film Festival this year for his contributions to film.

Entertainment.headline: Chase Stokes calls Morgan Evans 'pathetic excuse of masculinity' over Kelsea Ballerini divorce comments
Entertainment.detail: Chase Stokes criticized Morgan Evans' comments regarding his divorce from Kelsea Ballerini.

Entertainment.headline: Jamie Lee Curtis stuns in lingerie photo as fans say she's 'still got it'
Entertainment.detail: Jamie Lee Curtis posed in a lingerie photo, receiving positive reactions from fans.

Entertainment.headline: Lost and found: Madonna has the Spanish soccer jersey Celta Vigo was searching for
Entertainment.detail: Madonna is in possession of a Celta Vigo soccer jersey that the team was trying to locate.

Entertainment.headline: Ben Stiller slams White House for featuring Tropic Thunder clip in 'propaganda'
Entertainment.detail: Ben Stiller criticized the White House for using a clip from his movie 'Tropic Thunder' in what he considers propaganda.

Crime & Justice.headline: Former US intel director's daughter sentenced to 35 years for murder
Crime & Justice.detail: The daughter of a former U.S. intelligence director has been sentenced to 35 years in prison for murder.

Crime & Justice.headline: Phoenix Suns forward Dillon Brooks arrested for suspicion of DUI after traffic stop.
Crime & Justice.detail: Dillon Brooks, a forward for the Phoenix Suns, was arrested on suspicion of driving under the influence following a traffic stop.

Crime & Justice.headline: 4 men arrested in UK on suspicion of aiding Iran by spying on Jewish community.
Crime & Justice.detail: Four men have been arrested in the UK on suspicion of assisting Iran by gathering information on the Jewish community.

Crime & Justice.headline: Austin police release bodycam videos of officers locating shooting suspect
Crime & Justice.detail: Police in Austin released bodycam footage showing officers finding a shooting suspect, who was later killed by police, holding an AR-15 rifle.

Crime & Justice.headline: Ian Huntley, one of UK's most notorious child killers, has died, days after being attacked in prison.
Crime & Justice.detail: One of Britain's most notorious child killers has died, days after he was attacked by a fellow inmate in the workshop of a maximum security prison.

Education.headline: CHED, CFO Ink Deal to Strengthen Global Filipino Education
Education.detail: The Commission on Higher Education (CHED) has partnered with the Commission on Filipinos Overseas (CFO) to strengthen education opportunities for Filipinos living and working abroad, aiming to better guide, protect, and integrate Filipino students and young professionals into the global workforce. The CFO will share migration data with CHED to guide higher education policies and support Filipino students in international programs.

Education.headline: School Assembly News Headlines Today (March 08): Top National, Business News, Sports News, Education News & World News with Weather Updates & Thought of the Day
Education.detail: The Sunday Guardian provides a news roundup including education headlines such as the daughter of a carry bag supplier from Bihar securing AIR 185 in UPSC CSE 2025, and updates on weather and business. The headlines aim to keep students informed about key events in national, international, sports, and education sectors.

Education.headline: Free coding workshops for school girls
Education.detail: Flinders University is hosting free coding workshops for girls in Year 7-12 to promote computer programming skills and gender equity in technology. The Girls Programming Network (GPN) event offers targeted support for various skill levels and aims to connect girls with like-minded peers in STEM.

Education.headline: IT education to be incorporated into religious schools
Education.detail: The Malaysian government is set to incorporate information technology (IT) education into religious schools, focusing on digital skills alongside subjects like Bahasa Melayu, history, and religious curriculum. This initiative, spearheaded by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, aims to prepare students for technological developments.

Education.headline: OCHIENG: It's not too late to rethink subject pathways in secondary education
Education.detail: The Star reports on the need to rethink subject pathways in secondary education, advocating for students to take a mix of courses across sciences, humanities, and business. The article suggests moving away from rigid tracks in senior secondary school.

Lifestyle.headline: Frito-Lay recalls Miss Vickie's chips over potentially 'life-threatening' allergen risk
Lifestyle.detail: Frito-Lay recalled Miss Vickie's Spicy Dill Pickle chips over an undeclared milk allergen, posing a serious risk to those with dairy allergies.

Lifestyle.headline: Wendy's $100k 'Chief Tasting Officer' contest sparks hilarious fast-food social media spat
Lifestyle.detail: Wendy's launched a $100K Chief Tasting Officer contest running through the end of March as fast-food brands trade jabs on social media over their burgers.

Lifestyle.headline: Serena Williams Gets The Barbie Treatment For International Women's Day
Lifestyle.detail: In honor of International Women's Day, Mattel unveiled a one-of-a-kind Barbie doll inspired by the tennis legend.

Lifestyle.headline: Gayle King Signs New CBS Deal, Shutting Down Exit Rumors
Lifestyle.detail: The veteran journalist renewed her contract as CBS undergoes major changes under new leadership.

Lifestyle.headline: Brotherly Love: Photos Of Michael B. Jordan And His Brother Khalid Jordan
Lifestyle.detail: After a hilarious turn on Instagram, the spotlight is on baby bro, a handsome Howard alum and television personality.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Baseball mania returns to Taiwan
Opinion/Editorial.detail: With the World Baseball Classic underway and enthusiasm high since Taiwan's 2024 victory, hoodies with "Taiwan" are common, marking a surge in baseball interest.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Beijing has a three-body problem
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Analysts are questioning who will succeed Iran's supreme leader, but the real question is whether China has learned enough from the Persian Gulf to survive a war over Taiwan.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Mideast conflict not collapse of world order
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Commentaries suggest the US-Israel war on Iran has not tilted the world into "peril" or "disarray."

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Instead of spending billions on dementia care, try a brain workout
Opinion/Editorial.detail: New research highlights the benefits of brain training, adding to evidence that mentally stimulating activities can boost brain health.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The new age of warfare
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The US-Israel war on Iran has ushered in a new era of warfare, where precision-guided missiles and AI-driven surveillance have changed the battlefield, but modern technologies alone cannot win wars.

World.headline: Israel bombs Tehran oil depots; Iranian attacks on Gulf states continue
World.detail: Amid the ongoing conflict, Israel has targeted Tehran's oil depots while Iran continues its attacks on Gulf states, further escalating regional tensions.

World.headline: No sign of compromise in Middle East as Canadians flee U.S-Iran conflict
World.detail: Canadians are returning home from the Middle East as escalating U.S.-Iran tensions disrupt travel and rattle global energy markets.

World.headline: Gas Prices Surge in U.S. as Iran War Chokes Oil Supply
World.detail: The price of a gallon of gas has jumped 14% in a week and looks likely to rise even further as the war in Iran has disrupted oil supplies.

World.headline: New strikes light up the night in Tehran as Israel vows 'many surprises'
World.detail: Amid the ongoing war with Iran, Israel launched new strikes on Tehran and promised more to come.

World.headline: Michigan and Oklahoma tornado death toll rises to 8
World.detail: Severe storms caused a deadly tornado outbreak in Michigan and Oklahoma that has left at least 8 people dead and dozens injured.

Zimbabwe.headline: PSL hikes matchday gate charges
Zimbabwe.detail: The Premier Soccer League (PSL) has increased matchday gate charges for football matches.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zanu-PF Youth, join churches in CBD clean-up
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zanu-PF Youth League City Centre District collaborated with church leaders, residents, and NetOne staff for a clean-up initiative in the CBD.

Zimbabwe.headline: 21 600 Zimbabweans diagnosed with TB in 2025
Zimbabwe.detail: In 2025, 21,600 Zimbabweans were diagnosed with tuberculosis.

Zimbabwe.headline: No fans as Herentals host Chicken Inn
Zimbabwe.detail: Herentals will play without fans when they host Chicken Inn due to disciplinary sanctions.

Zimbabwe.headline: Division One Officiating First Step Towards PSL
Zimbabwe.detail: A senior soccer referees' body member, Edson Nkau, says Division One officiating is the final step towards being a Zifa Panelist which enables one to officiate in the Premier Soccer League.
